//: AUTO-GENERATED — do not edit this file. Source of truth: checks.yaml. Regenerate: npx sentinel update

guides.dev-note: This repo is simultaneously the Sentinel codebase and its own guinea pig. Every check below runs against Sentinel itself. When an AI agent adds a new persistent check (new linter, new validator, new invariant), add it here so all future agents inherit it automatically.

deterministic.label: All YAML templates parse against Zod schema
deterministic.cmd: node --input-type=module -e "import {parseSentinelYaml} from './packages/yaml-core/dist/index.js';import {readFileSync,readdirSync} from 'node:fs';const f=readdirSync('templates').filter(f=>f.endsWith('.yaml'));for(const t of f){parseSentinelYaml(readFileSync('templates/'+t,'utf8'));console.log('OK: '+t);}console.log('All '+f.length+' templates valid.');"

deterministic.label: Sentinel engine files in sync with checks.yaml

manual.label: Rebuild dist/ after any source change
manual.manual: After changing any package source file in packages/*/src/, run: pnpm turbo build The CLI and engines run from dist/, not src/ — stale builds silently run old code.

manual.label: Keep when patterns in sync: yaml-core ↔ engine-copilot
manual.manual: If you changed scope patterns in packages/yaml-core/src/trigger.ts, mirror the same file coverage in packages/engine-copilot/src/engine.ts (the matchesWhen() regex arrays inside the generated extension.mjs string).

manual.label: Update Zod schema when TypeScript types change
manual.manual: If you changed interfaces in packages/types/src/index.ts, update the Zod schema in packages/yaml-core/src/schema.ts to match, then rebuild: pnpm turbo build.

manual.label: Sync Toolbar.tsx INLINE_TEMPLATES with templates/*.yaml
manual.manual: If you changed any file in templates/*.yaml, update the matching entry in apps/builder/src/components/Toolbar.tsx INLINE_TEMPLATES — same IDs, labels, commands, manual text, and conditions.

manual.label: Update README when CLI commands change
manual.manual: If you added, removed, or changed a command in packages/cli/src/commands/, update the Commands section in README.md with the new usage and description.
